title: Ensure Oslogin Is Enabled for a Project

input_row.title: Ensure Oslogin Is Enabled for a Project
input_row.description: Enabling OS login binds SSH certificates to IAM users and facilitates effective SSH certificate management.
input_row.rationale: Enabling osLogin ensures that SSH keys used to connect to instances are mapped with IAM users. Revoking access to IAM user will revoke all the SSH keys associated with that particular user. It facilitates centralized and automated SSH key pair management which is useful in handling cases like response to compromised SSH key pairs and/or revocation of external/third-party/Vendor users.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the VM compute metadata page by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/compute/metadata. 2. Ensure that key enable-oslogin is present with value set to TRUE. 3. Because instances can override project settings, ensure that no instance has custom metadata with key enable-oslogin and value FALSE. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List the instances in your project and get details on each instance: gcloud compute instances list --format=json 2. Verify that the section commonInstanceMetadata has a key enable-oslogin set to value TRUE. Exception: VMs created by GKE should be excluded. These VMs have names that start with gke- and are labeled goog-gke-node
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the VM compute metadata page by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/compute/metadata. 2. Click Edit. 3. Add a metadata entry where the key is enable-oslogin and the value is TRUE. 4. Click Save to apply the changes. 5. For every instance that overrides the project setting, go to the VM Instances page at https://console.cloud.google.com/compute/instances. 6. Click the name of the instance on which you want to remove the metadata value. 7. At the top of the instance details page, click Edit to edit the instance settings. 8. Under Custom metadata, remove any entry with key enable-oslogin and the value is FALSE 9. At the bottom of the instance details page, click Save to apply your changes to the instance. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Configure oslogin on the project: gcloud compute project-info add-metadata --metadata enable-oslogin=TRUE 2. Remove instance metadata that overrides the project setting. gcloud compute instances remove-metadata --keys=enable- oslogin Optionally, you can enable two factor authentication for OS login. For more information, see: https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/oslogin/setup-two-factor-authentication.
input_row.impact: Enabling OS Login on project disables metadata-based SSH key configurations on all instances from a project. Disabling OS Login restores SSH keys that you have configured in project or instance meta-data.
input_row.default_value: By default, parameter enable-oslogin is not set, which is equivalent to setting it to FALSE.
input_row.additional_information: 1. In order to use osLogin, instance using Custom Images must have the latest version of the Linux Guest Environment installed. The following image families do not yet support OS Login: Project cos-cloud (Container-Optimized OS) image family cos-stable. All project coreos-cloud (CoreOS) image families Project suse-cloud (SLES) image family sles-11 All Windows Server and SQL Server image families 2. Project enable-oslogin can be over-ridden by setting enable-oslogin parameter to an instance metadata individually.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit approach is Automated due to the following criteria being met:
- GCP APIs can query this property as it pertains to project-level metadata settings for enabling OS Login.
- The audit section mentions Google Cloud CLI commands which can retrieve necessary metadata configurations.
- The nature of the control pertains to IAM and VM instance metadata, which is accessible programmatically.
- The change in project-wide settings does not necessitate manual inspection of instance configurations since the relevant metadata can be checked directly via the API calls and `gcloud` commands outlined in the audit field.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_compute_project_os_login_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **GCP API calls or Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) methods to use:**
   - Use `gcloud compute project-info describe` command to fetch the project metadata and check if `enable-oslogin` is set to TRUE.

2. **GCP CLI commands:**
   - Command: `gcloud compute project-info describe --project [PROJECT_ID] --format=json`
   - After obtaining the project metadata, check the `commonInstanceMetadata` section for `enable-oslogin` key.

3. **Specific resources to enumerate:**
   - Iterate through all VM instances: `gcloud compute instances list --format=json`
   - Check each VM instance to ensure there is no custom metadata overriding the project setting (i.e., `enable-oslogin` key set to FALSE).

4. **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
   - Verify that the key `enable-oslogin` is present and set to TRUE in the project's metadata.
   - Ensure that no individual VM instance metadata has `enable-oslogin` set to FALSE.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
   - Pass Criteria: 
     - Project metadata contains `enable-oslogin` set to TRUE.
     - No VM instance metadata has `enable-oslogin` set to FALSE.
   - Fail Criteria: 
     - Project metadata does not contain `enable-oslogin` or it's set to FALSE.
     - Any VM instance has its `enable-oslogin` overridden with a value of FALSE.

This automated approach effectively ensures compliance with the CIS GCP control regarding OS Login, providing a clear pass/fail mechanism through existing GCP APIs and CLI commands.
